content_uniqueness.detail: Both pages share identical section structure: hero-section, social-proof-bar-section, problem-section, solution-features-section, testimonials-section, details-section, final-cta-section. Same section names, same order, same count (7 each). Section labels are 100% identical between pages.

brief_as_content.detail: Brief instructions rendered verbatim as page content instead of being used as a generation prompt.

evidence.landing_h1: Build a modern, conversion-focused landing page for this business
evidence.landing_subheadline: Include these sections in order: (1) A hero section with a compelling headline about the product's core value, a supporting subheadline, a primary CTA button, a secondary CTA button, and a background visual or gradient.
evidence.service_h1: Build a content-rich service detail page
evidence.service_subheadline: Include these sections in order: (1) A hero section with a clear service headline and supporting subheadline.
landing_text_elements_with_brief: Include these sections in order: (1) A hero section...
service_text_elements_with_brief: Include these sections in order: (1) A hero section...
key benefits as icons or cards

extra_sections: problem (not explicitly requested as separate section)

brand_color_check.detail: Site style memory is not configured, so no brand colors were stored. The build used a consistent dark navy palette (#1E3A5F) which matches the existing site header, but this is coincidental inheritance from the site template rather than deliberate brand compliance. Hero bg: rgb(30, 58, 95). Section alternation: #f1f5f9 / #f8fafc. Text color: rgb(51, 65, 85).

cta_text_check.detail: Site style memory not configured — no stored CTA text to compare. Both pages used 'Get Started' (primary) and 'Contact Us' (secondary). These are generic, not derived from any stored brand values.

image_count.detail: Zero content images on either page. Only image is the header logo (from template). The build tool did not call search-stock-images or import-stock-image.

overall_fidelity_verdict: CRITICAL FAILURE — The build tool rendered brief instructions as literal page content. Both pages are structurally identical despite requesting different layouts. No real content was generated.
